title: NOC 75212 – Public works and maintenance labourers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 75212 professionals are the essential guardians of Canada’s municipal and provincial infrastructure. As a TEER 5 occupation, these labourers perform the manual work required to maintain roads, bridges, sewers, and public spaces. In 2026, as Canada invests heavily in "Climate-Resilient Infrastructure" and urban greening projects, these workers are classified as Essential Municipal Talent. For immigration, this role is a key target for the Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot and the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level5Major Group75 – Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourersSub-Major Group752 – Other transport drivers, operators and related occupationsMinor Group7521 – Boat and cable ferry operators and related occupationsNOC Code75212

duties: Road Maintenance: Fill potholes, spread sand or salt on icy roads, and assist in the repair of sidewalks, curbs, and gutters.
Sewer & Water Support: Dig trenches for water and sewer pipes; clean and maintain catch basins and manholes.
Signage & Safety: Help install and maintain traffic signs and barriers; paint crosswalks and lane markings.
Waste Management: Assist in the collection of refuse and recyclable materials; maintain public waste bins in parks and urban areas.
Seasonal Operations: Operate snowblowers, shovels, and salt spreaders during winter months; perform landscaping and park maintenance in summer.
Equipment Assistance: Assist operators of heavy equipment such as pavers, graders, and sweepers by providing manual labor and signaling.
Cleaning & Upkeep: Clean debris from roads, bridges, and public tunnels; perform general maintenance on municipal buildings and grounds.
employment_requirements: No formal education is typically required.
On-the-Job Training: Extensive on-the-job training is provided by the employing municipality or contractor.

Certifications (Role Specific):A valid Class 5 Driver’s License is often a mandatory requirement.WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) is a standard safety requirement.Traffic Control Person (TCP) certification may be required for road-side work.
A valid Class 5 Driver’s License is often a mandatory requirement.
WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) is a standard safety requirement.
Traffic Control Person (TCP) certification may be required for road-side work.
Physical strength, stamina for outdoor work in all weather conditions (from -30°C to +30°C), and a safety-first mindset are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, unionized municipal labourers in major cities often earn annual salaries between $55,000 and $72,000 inclusive of comprehensive benefits and overtime premiums for emergency repairs.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the shift toward "Smart Cities"—utilizing digital sensors for water and road management—will require more technical support from maintenance crews, but the core manual labor for infrastructure repair cannot be automated. According to COPS, "Replacement Demand" will create over 20,000 job openings across Canada through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for public works labourers in 2026 is Strong and Consistent, particularly in rapidly growing urban centers and northern communities. In Ontario, British Columbia, and Alberta, municipal governments are facing a "Replacement Crisis" as over 25% of the current maintenance workforce reaches retirement age. According to 2026 municipal budget reports, there is an urgent need for workers capable of supporting year-round maintenance, from summer road paving to winter snow removal operations.
Public Sector Stability: Many roles are within municipal or provincial governments, offering superior job security, union protection, and long-term pension plans.
Rapid Workforce Entry: This role typically requires no formal education or prior experience, making it the fastest way for newcomers to enter a stable, government-supported career path.
Pathways to Specialization: Many labourers successfully transition into high-paying roles as Heavy Equipment Operators, Water Treatment Technicians, or Public Works Foremen.
Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 5 role is a primary beneficiary of Provincial Nominee Programs (PNP) that prioritize "Essential Community Infrastructure" workers.
pathways: Saskatchewan SINP (Hard-to-Fill Skills): A high-priority stream for public works labourers with a valid job offer in the province.
New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A dedicated stream for 33,000 workers already in Canada, targeting "Essential Community Service and Infrastructure" roles.
OINP In-Demand Skills Stream: Frequently targets labourers in regions outside the Greater Toronto Area (GTA) for municipal contracts.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion route for those with a job offer from a designated municipal employer in an Atlantic province.
